title: Cornmeal Crusted Salmon Fillets with Artichoke Shallot Sauce
ingredients: 1 cup canned small artichoke hearts, coarsely chopped
1 cup chicken or vegetable broth or 1 cup non-alcoholic or regular white wine
2 tbsp. capers with a bit of juice
Parmesan Garlic Mashed Potatoes for serving
instructions: Wash and pat dry salmon fillets.
In a medium bowl or on parchment paper, combine cornmeal, paprika, salt, pepper, and dill weed.
Dredge salmon fillets in cornmeal mixture, coating all sides well.
Heat ½ inch vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
When oil is hot, add salmon fillets and cook until cornmeal crust is golden.
Turn fillets over and cook until golden on the other side (salmon should not be cooked through at this stage).
Place seared fillets on a baking sheet and bake at 350°F for 5-8 minutes depending on thickness.
For the sauce, heat butter in a saucepan.
Add chopped shallots and cook for 2 minutes until softened.
Add artichoke hearts, lemon zest and juice, broth or wine, capers with juice, chives, salt, and pepper.
To plate, serve salmon on top of Parmesan Garlic Mashed Potatoes and spoon artichoke shallot sauce on top.
Garnish with a lemon wedge dipped in chopped fresh parsley.
notes: Dredged salmon fillets can be prepared ahead and refrigerated.
The searing step cooks only the outside coating; the salmon will finish cooking in the oven.
Cooking time in oven varies depending on salmon thickness (5-8 minutes).
This artichoke shallot sauce is also delicious on other fish and pork tenderloin.
